hero.heading: Your Trusted Guide To Studying in Australia
hero.subheading: Global Educational Pathways — personal, QEAC-certified education counselling
hero.paragraph: A small, family-run consultancy, not a call centre. Founder Sabina Yasmin personally guides every student from choosing the right course through to visa approval and settling in.

whyChooseUs.heading: Why Study Abroad Applicants Choose Global Educational Pathways
whyChooseUs.paragraph: Global Educational Pathways is led by Sabina Yasmin, a Qualified Education Agent Counsellor (QEAC) certified by ICEF Academy and a working professor at Sydney Imperial Institute. She has lived the exact journey her students are about to take — arriving in Australia, rebuilding a career from scratch, and learning the system from the inside. Backed by a small family team spanning Bangladesh and Sydney, every student gets direct, personal attention — never a rotating cast of junior staff.

services.heading: How We Help
services.paragraph: Support at every stage of the journey to study in Australia.

items.title: Primary Assessment
items.paragraph: A free, honest evaluation of your academic background, finances, and English proficiency to find Australian programs you're genuinely eligible for.

items.title: Application Processing
items.paragraph: Hands-on help preparing and submitting university applications, statements of purpose, and tracking every response.

items.title: Documentation
items.paragraph: Guidance on academic records, financial proof, and Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) documents so nothing holds up your file.

items.title: Visa Application & Interview
items.paragraph: Preparation for the Subclass 500 student visa, including GTE statements, financial evidence, and interview readiness.

items.title: Post-Arrival Support
items.paragraph: With a genuinely Sydney-based team, we can offer in-person help after you land — not just remote advice.

studyAbroadChecklist.heading: Study Abroad Checklist
items: Free Assessment: Get your academic, financial, and language profile assessed before you commit to a plan.
University Shortlist: Decide your preferred field of study and shortlist Australian universities/colleges that fit.
Document Collection: Gather transcripts, certificates, financial records, and identification.
English Proficiency: Prepare for and sit IELTS, PTE, or another accepted English test.
Application Deadlines: Apply at least 6 months ahead of your target intake — earlier for specialised courses.
University Application: Submit a complete, accurate application with all supporting documents.
Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE): Secure your CoE once accepted, ready to support your visa application.
Visa Application: Lodge your student visa application with financial and GTE documentation in order.
Pre-Departure Preparation: Arrange travel, accommodation, and health cover ahead of arrival.
Arrival in Australia: Land with a plan — and, where needed, in-person support already in place.

items.question: Do students have to pay Global Educational Pathways for its services?
items.answer: No. We do not charge students or families any fee. Our service is funded through incentives paid by partner universities and colleges when a student successfully enrols — the same way most licensed education agents operate. Our recommendations are not influenced by which institution pays more; the goal is matching each student to the right fit.

items.question: What makes Global Educational Pathways different from a large consultancy?
items.answer: We're a small, family-run team, not a rotating cast of junior counsellors. Our founder is a certified QEAC and a working academic in Australia, and every student is supported by people who are personally invested in the outcome — from first assessment through to arrival.

items.question: Which countries do you help students apply to?
items.answer: Australia only. Specialising in a single destination lets us stay deeply current on Australian visa rules, university requirements, and living conditions, rather than spreading our expertise thin across many countries.

items.question: Can you help with English proficiency test preparation?
items.answer: We can advise on which test (IELTS, PTE, etc.) fits your target course and refer you to trusted preparation resources, though test coaching itself is not our core service.

items.question: Where is Global Educational Pathways based?
items.answer: We operate across Bangladesh and Sydney, Australia. Our founder is a professor at Sydney Imperial Institute and part of our team lives permanently in Sydney, meaning we can offer in-person support after students arrive — not just remote guidance.
